game_info.title: Touch Cricket - Arcade Batting Challenge
game_info.concept: **Touch Cricket:** Touch Cricket is a batting simulation where players control a batsman, utilizing multi-touch gestures to execute shots against various bowling deliveries. The core mechanic involves precisely timing a combination of taps for footwork (e.g., a single tap for front foot, double tap for back foot) and directional swipes (e.g., swipe up for a straight drive, left for a cover drive) to hit the ball effectively. Each delivery, from 80 km/h fast balls to spinning leg breaks, requires a specific footwork and shot combination, with mistimed or incorrect inputs increasing the chance of getting out. Players accumulate runs by hitting the ball into scoring zones, aiming to build a high score before losing 10 wickets, which ends the innings. Progression occurs through a Career Mode, where players advance from club to international level by achieving specific run targets and winning matches across Twenty20, ODI, and Test formats, with difficulty scaling via increasingly skilled AI bowlers and varied pitch conditions.
game_info.description: Touch Cricket is a fast-paced batting simulation where you face increasingly challenging bowling deliveries. Time your shots perfectly by combining footwork and directional strokes to score runs. Each ball requires precise timing - mistimed shots can lead to getting out. Build your score while managing your 10 wickets across different delivery types including fast balls, spinners, and yorkers. Survive and score big to progress through career levels!
game_info.controls:
Arrow Up/W: Front Foot Shot
Arrow Down/S: Back Foot Shot
Arrow Left/A: Off Side Shot (Left)
Arrow Right/D: On Side Shot (Right)
Space: Defensive Block
Shift: Power Shot (Hold with direction)
Z: Late Cut/Flick Shot
Enter: Start Game
Esc: Pause/Unpause
R: Restart (from Game Over)

TEST_1.test_description: Testing basic batting mechanics and ball delivery system. Verifies that the player can execute different shot types (front foot, back foot, defensive) and that the ball is bowled correctly with realistic physics. Ensures collision detection works between bat and ball.
TEST_1.strategy_description: Automatically execute a sequence of different shot types: front foot drive (W), back foot defense (S), off side shot (A), on side shot (D), and defensive block (Space). Wait for ball delivery, then execute shot at optimal timing. Repeat for 20 deliveries to test variety.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: All shot types should execute without errors. Ball should be bowled consistently with visible trajectory. Successful hits should result in the ball traveling in the intended direction. Score should increase for good shots. No crashes or physics glitches should occur.

TEST_2.test_description: Testing win condition by scoring runs efficiently. Verifies that the game correctly tracks runs, wickets, and progresses to win state when target is achieved. Tests the game over flow for winning scenario.
TEST_2.strategy_description: Execute optimal shots for each delivery type - front foot drives (W+D) for full deliveries, back foot cuts (S+A) for short balls, power shots (Shift+W) when possible. Aim for boundaries (4s and 6s) by timing perfectly. Continue aggressive batting to reach 50 runs quickly.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: Player should consistently score runs with good shot selection. Game should transition to GAME_OVER_WIN state upon reaching 50 runs with wickets remaining. Victory screen should display final score and statistics. R key should properly restart the game.

TEST_3.test_description: Testing lose condition and wicket system. Verifies that mistimed shots, wrong footwork, and missed balls correctly result in dismissals. Ensures game ends when all 10 wickets are lost.
TEST_3.strategy_description: Intentionally play poor shots - use back foot (S) for full deliveries, front foot (W) for short balls, miss timing by pressing keys too early or too late. Let some balls pass without playing a shot to test bowled dismissals.
TEST_3.expected_outcome: Mistimed or incorrect shots should result in wicket loss. Wicket counter should decrease correctly. Game should transition to GAME_OVER_LOSE when all 10 wickets are lost. Lose screen should display with option to restart.

TEST_4.test_description: Testing different delivery types and their unique characteristics. Verifies that fast balls, spinners, yorkers, and bouncers behave distinctly and require different shot techniques.
TEST_4.expected_outcome: Each delivery type should have distinct visual and physical characteristics. Yorkers should be low and fast, bouncers should bounce high, spinners should have curve. Correct shot selection should yield better results than incorrect selection for each type.
TEST_4.description: Testing different delivery types and their unique characteristics. Verifies that fast balls, spinners, yorkers, and bouncers behave distinctly and require different shot techniques.
TEST_4.expected: Each delivery type should have distinct visual and physical characteristics. Yorkers should be low and fast, bouncers should bounce high, spinners should have curve. Correct shot selection should yield better results than incorrect selection for each type.

TEST_5.test_description: Testing power shot mechanics and risk-reward balance. Verifies that holding Shift while executing shots increases power but also increases risk of getting out if mistimed.
TEST_5.strategy_description: Alternate between normal shots and power shots (Shift+direction keys). Execute power shots with perfect timing and also with poor timing. Compare run scoring and dismissal rates between normal and power shots.
TEST_5.expected_outcome: Power shots should travel faster and farther than normal shots when timed well, scoring more runs (boundaries). Mistimed power shots should have higher chance of dismissal. Visual feedback should indicate power shot activation.

TEST_6.test_description: Testing pause and resume functionality. Verifies that ESC key properly pauses the game, freezes physics, and resumes without issues. Ensures game state is preserved during pause.
TEST_6.strategy_description: Start game, score some runs, press ESC to pause during ball delivery. Verify game freezes. Press ESC again to resume. Repeat pause/resume cycle multiple times during different game situations (ball in air, between deliveries).
TEST_6.expected_outcome: ESC should transition between PLAYING and PAUSED states smoothly. Physics should freeze during pause. Score, wickets, and ball position should be preserved. Game should resume exactly where it paused without any jumps or glitches.

TEST_7.test_description: Testing game restart functionality and state reset. Verifies that pressing R from game over screen properly resets all game state and returns to start screen for a fresh game.
TEST_7.expected_outcome: R key from game over should reset score to 0, wickets to 10, and clear all entities. Game should return to START screen. All game state should be clean for new game. Multiple restart cycles should work without memory leaks or accumulated errors.
